---
| CUT TO THE CHASE WITH SENATOR CHASE Weekly Session Newsletter Week 4 of 7
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| |
---
| WHAT HAPPENED THIS WEEK?
---
| WEEK 4: GENERAL ASSEMBLY UPDATE
---
| |
---
| Senator Amanda Chase
---
Senator Chase has represented Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia Counties in the Senate since first elected in 2015, almost 8 years.
She has been rated as the most conservative Senator in Virginia by the American Conservative Union and has a lifetime score of 100% with the Family Foundation, VCDL and A with the NRA.
She is also a champion in the General Assembly for pushing back against vaccine and mask mandates, calling for a full investigation into the 2020 Presidential Election, parental rights in education, medical freedom, protecting gun rights and help for our veterans.
---
| 2023 SESSION WEEK 4 OVERVIEW "The Hunger Games"

Dear Friends, It's been a tough week. I had to play catch up after being out sick with Covid for 3 days. But thanks to my hardworking team, we did it. I was back last Thursday presenting bills.
Some days, we felt like we were living an episode out of The Hunger Games. Despite every obstacle that got thrown our way, with God's help, we prevailed.
This week I presented all of my bills from last week plus the ones scheduled for this week. As of Friday, I will have presented all 19 bills just in time for crossover, which is next Wednesday, February 8. You can click [here ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001lptxhaQT_idHd5p0T0Y5o6szSWfwSste8PeUNm7TOL34mW3J7QfYBoow48vhZzSRC3b9PA3jWe5ybjvFIaB7i4swF3EdAaPoLPmZEaCSbHSkziulr0bIEWq9pUvGz8lHuvh-MzZCcVWFPKP-xgZtS3cRTWvmsk6vI6JSzuJIIc4kwXK6DOGBbgm8QWEwlsSFj37qtgHDbWE=&c=BzwnkIGP1A0lfVtTkUpq0RD7FR9wHvPtFc-mGevIpsh6MkFAxx7tlg==&ch=XWOAwuYUL2d1XEQTEqZJ1rwiabC5KeGWeX_g6DTs0Y3WmdAcyLY8Fw==)for a list of those bills.
Fortunately, all of my bills were heard in the Senate. Unfortunately, they all died in Democrat controlled committees. In the Senate Privileges and Elections Committee, the Senate Health and Education Committee, the Judiciary Committee the committees are stacked against 9 Democrats to 6 Republicans or worse yet Senate Commerce and Labor with 12 Democrats to 3 Republicans.
Clearly, Democrats do not support ending Covid mandates, school choice, protecting innocent life, protecting the 2nd Amendment or election integrity. In order to pass these bills, we're going to have to win back the Virginia Senate this year.
The only clear solution is to elect more Republicans to the Virginia Senate this coming June 20 in the Republican Primary and on November 7 in the General Election. Currently, the Virginia Senate is 22 Democrats to 18 Republicans. We must regain control of the Senate if we're going to pass meaningful legislation that is important to you.
All Senate bills must be heard before Tuesday at midnight, or they die. Beginning Wednesday, we will only vote on bills that passed out of the House. Beginning next Wednesday, my focus will be House bills.
Unfortunately, all 19 of my bills died at the hands of Democrat controlled Senate Committees. This came as no surprise to us and was our expectation. While it's great to have your bills pass, it's more important in my opinion, to introduce bills that truly impact people's lives in a meaningful way. Otherwise, what's the point?
Every time you present a bill, you are given an opportunity to educate and inform stakeholders, fellow legislators and move the needle closer to passing meaningful legislation once your party is back in control. Plus you are forcing votes on important issues, which is important right before election season.
Even though I've been in the Senate for 8 years, I haven't forgotten who I represent. I represent you, not the special interest groups and lobbyists. All 19 of the bills that I introduced this year came from you. While we may have to wait next year to successfully advance these issues, we are primed and ready once we do take back the Senate.
In this week's newsletter, I provide an overview of the fourth week of the 2023 General Assembly Session. I've presented well over 11 bills in the past 2 weeks. Included for you in this newsletter are overviews and even some videos of bills that I presented in committee along with comments I've made regarding certain bills on the Senate floor.
There are links at the bottom of the newsletter that will help you follow various bills, legislators, committees and how to reach my office.
If you'd like to stop by the General Assembly, I encourage you to reach out to my office below. We'd love to have you. If you call in advance we will do our best to find a time that I can personally meet with you.
Also, if you'd like to help out, we're looking for people to collect signatures so that I can get my name on the ballot. I need 250 signatures. If you're interested, please reach out to Debbie at 804 393 2715.
Please keep us in your prayers as we enter crossover next week. We hope to see you soon!
---
| Chase Bills SB805 and SB1488
---
| Repealing the Unconstitutional Gun Grab of 2020
---
| | |
---
| Senator Chase Presents SB 805 Reverse Gun Free Zones by Localities
---
|
---
|
---
| Senator Chase Presents SB1488 Reverse Red Flag Laws
---
| Senator Amanda Chase presented both SB1488 to Reverse Unconstitutional and Unamerican "Red Flag" Laws and SB805 to Reverse Locality Control of Gun Free Zones before the Senate Judiciary Committee Monday morning at 8am in the Senate Judiciary Committee.
Both bills seek to reverse the Gun Grab that took place in 2020. Unfortunately, both bills died in the Senate Judiciary Committee with the help of Democrats and Republican Minority Leader Senator Tommy Norment who voted with Democrats in favor of gun control on both bills.
SB1488 is a Chase sponsored bill to Reverse Unconstitutional "Red Flag" Laws which fail to recognize that the government should NOT seize weapons from people who haven't been convicted of a crime.
"Individuals have a Constitutional right to protect the health and well being of both themselves and their family from violence that has overtaken many peaceful Virginia communities. Many suburban moms refuse to be victims in our crime ridden neighborhoods and choose to conceal carry to protect both themselves and their family. When seconds matter, we will do whatever it takes to protect our children from criminals. Once again, the Virginia Democrat Party violates the Constitution, restricts our gun rights and in 2020 passed unconstitutional laws. It's time to draw a line in the sand and take back what is ours." stated Senator Chase.
Even the New York State Supreme Court agrees. Red Flag laws are unconstitutional. They allow the government to take away our guns without any due process for up to 14 days and potentially longer, placing the burden on the unsuspecting individual who has not committed a crime. This is unamerican. What happened to innocent until proven guilty? Red flag laws allow individuals who are not medically trained to deny an individual their basic fundamental right to protect themselves despite the fact that the person hasn't committed a crime," continued Senator Amanda Chase.
The State Supreme Court judge in Rochester, New York ruled this past December that Red Flag laws are unconstitutional.
"The Courts agreed with Colorado local district attorney Michael Allen who when the Democrats came after our gun rights in 2020 said, "Red flag laws are not only unconstitutional, they are a poor excuse to take people's guns and not designed in any way to address real mental health concerns.'"
"It's time to honor the U.S. Constitution and strike these unconstitutional laws that are mischievous at best, allowing individuals to take away another individual's rights based on speculation and hearsay," stated Senator Chase.

Senator Chase is also presented SB805 to reverse a Virginia law that was passed in 2020 by the Democrat controlled General Assembly which allows a locality to create gun free zones.
Gun free zones disarm law abiding citizens who can no longer legally protect themselves. Democrat controlled cities across the state of Virginia have now passed a confusing and burdensome patchwork of regulations and ordinances which vary from city to city and county to county. This makes traveling difficult for even the most conscientious conceal carry holder to follow, increasing the risk that they could unintentionally and inadvertently violate the ordinance while traveling across the Commonwealth. We should not make it more difficult for law abiding citizens to follow the law.
Gun free zones empower criminals and increase gun violence as criminals take advantage of disarmed law abiding citizens. Gun free zones increase the risk of guns being stolen from vehicles when conceal carry holders are forced to disarm and store their weapon in their vehicle to enter an event. Even guns locked in cases and stored in a vehicle can be stolen from an unattended vehicle. It is better for the conceal carry holder to be in possession of their weapon.
"Gun free zones do nothing more than make law abiding citizens sitting ducks or fish in a barrel. Gun free zones make law abiding citizens more vulnerable and unable to protect themselves from criminals who have no regard for our laws. It doesn't matter how many gun control bills the legislature passes, they only embolden and empower criminals who don't follow restrictive gun laws, " stated Senator Amanda Chase.
Senator Chase continued, "Over the past nearly two years, we have seen a troubling trend take place with Democrat controlled legislatures and localities across Virginia, who have mistreated and abused law enforcement while coddling violent murderers and criminals, while simultaneously misusing their authority to override both the U.S. Constitution and the Virginia Constitution to allow law abiding citizens to protect their family. Both state "'the people have the right of to keep and bear Arms, and that right shall not be infringed.'"
Laws that infringe upon our federally protected Constitutional rights should be struck down. Virginia's very own Constitution protects the rights of Virginian's to protect themselves.
The U.S. Constitution clearly states, "A well regulated Militia, being necessary to the security of a free State, the right of the people to keep and bear Arms, shall not be infringed."
The Virginia Constitution in Article I of the Bill of Rights in Section 13 states, "That a well regulated militia, composed of the body of the people, trained to arms, is the proper, natural, and safe defense of a free state, therefore, the right of the people to keep and bear arms shall not be infringed; that standing armies, in time of peace, should be avoided as dangerous to liberty; and that in all cases the military should be under strict subordination to, and governed by, the civil power."
"The Gun Grab of 2020 must be reversed. It violates our basic fundamental 2nd Amendment right to protect ourselves and I will work diligently until the Gun Grab of 2020 has been completely reversed," stated Senator Chase.

| Chase Bill SB849 Smart Meters
---
| Making Sure Dominion Energy Asks You Before They Install a Smart Meter
---
| Are Homeowners Being Fully Educated and Informed about the Potential Dangers of Smart Meters that Dominion Is Installing on Homes; Many Homeowners Are Saying Without Permission?
SB849 Chase Bill Requires Dominion Energy to Receive Written Homeowner Consent Before Installing Smart Meters

Senator Amanda Chase presented SB849 Relating to Public Utilities, Customer Consent to Install Wireless Smart Meters Monday Afternoon Before A Senate Commerce and Labor Committee. The Bill Died in Committee.

Senator Amanda Chase presented [SB849](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001lptxhaQT_idHd5p0T0Y5o6szSWfwSste8PeUNm7TOL34mW3J7QfYBh8hPfUWSHooKznb5OHvyp7gP2cRW2A9oPbHwU1oyy5DX8fRxspvvwmtyGQd7kwvKeOtIZbA1-wikDn3TdGc6nUaa4OdbHGiNXERZG14WKRK5qvraBwP0omhljushH8R8e6t97tF_IiooXTsfGy0Ge4=&c=BzwnkIGP1A0lfVtTkUpq0RD7FR9wHvPtFc-mGevIpsh6MkFAxx7tlg==&ch=XWOAwuYUL2d1XEQTEqZJ1rwiabC5KeGWeX_g6DTs0Y3WmdAcyLY8Fw==) before the Senate Commerce and Labor Committee, Monday afternoon in an effort to ensure consumers reserve the right to decline without penalty wireless Smart Meters being installed on their home or business. The bill failed in committee 14 to 1.
Note that every legislator on this committee receives thousand of dollars in donations from Dominion Energy. Senator Chase does not. Don't take my word for it. See for yourself using the links below. Legislators should not be accepting money from a utility monopoly whose SCC judges we appoint.
SB849 Relating to Public Utilities, Customer Consent to Install Wireless Smart Meters recognizes that Dominion Energy or any other utility or 3rd party vendor should NOT install wireless Smart Meters without express written permission from the homeowner or business owner, who should be given full information about any potential dangers or risks associated with the meter and the right to decline without penalty if the customer decides against having the smart meter installed.
SB849 Prohibits a public utility from installing a wireless smart meter, defined in the bill, on the premises of a customer without first obtaining such customer's consent to such installation. The bill requires a public utility, for any wireless smart meter installed by a public utility prior to July 1, 2023, to provide the customer an opportunity to consent to the previous installation.
The bill requires a public utility to offer to remove a wireless smart meter without cost to the customer that was installed without obtaining the customer's consent.
The bill prohibits a public utility from (i) discontinuing service to a customer who does not consent to the installation of a wireless smart meter; (ii) charging a fee, assessment, or higher rate to a customer who does not consent to the installation of a wireless smart meter; or (iii) providing or offering to provide discounted rates to a customer in exchange for obtaining the customer's consent to install a smart meter.
"Customers have the right to make well informed and conscientious decisions that could potentially effect the health and well being of both themselves, their family, their employees and their customers from known and unknown risks associated with the use of wireless Smart Meters, without penalty. All we're asking for here is informed consent, full information and acceptable alternatives when the consumer declines. " Senator Amanda Chase
THE VOTE SB 849 Public utilities; customer consent to install smart meter.

| Chase Bill SB884 The Voter Confidence Restoration Act
---
| It's Time to Put the Guardrails Back on our Virginia Elections
---
| Most Virginians Don't Trust That We Have Safe, Secure Elections SB884 Chase-sponsored Voter Confidence Restoration Act Attempts to Restore That Confidence Died on a Party Line Vote

SB884 Chase Bill Restores Voter Confidence By Putting the Guardrails Back on Virginia's Elections to include Eliminating Voter Computers and Tabulators

Senator Amanda Chase presented SB884 The Voter Confidence Restoration Act, This Tuesday Afternoon at the Capitol, Before a Senate Privileges & Elections Committee

Virginia Senator Amanda Chase presented [SB884](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001lptxhaQT_idHd5p0T0Y5o6szSWfwSste8PeUNm7TOL34mW3J7QfYBsK7M8Mh8Fyd9NYaMFfWDnlS4i11GGDCajdF9PEE-Ln_EtNaNyONgwEeCjDvOu9NlIIzlROUL6xn2nYsinNlO6IkZSb9n-L64RA0sIPpxdoX3xI0ON9QnJpV28eUWpPDxk5GPV_mzeUWzBZz54LHyac=&c=BzwnkIGP1A0lfVtTkUpq0RD7FR9wHvPtFc-mGevIpsh6MkFAxx7tlg==&ch=XWOAwuYUL2d1XEQTEqZJ1rwiabC5KeGWeX_g6DTs0Y3WmdAcyLY8Fw==) An Election Integrity Omnibus bill called The Voter Confidence Restoration Act before the Senate Privileges and Elections Committee, Tuesday afternoon in an effort to restore Virginians' confidence in their elections.
SB884 The Voter Confidence Restoration Act recognizes that most Virginians don't trust our election processes and therefore the results. This bill understands the importance of restoring that confidence by getting back to the basics of election integrity and ensuring safe, transparent elections that all voters can trust.
SB884 addresses many of the concerns voters experienced during the 2020 Presidential Election and seeks to ensure these issues never happen again.
The Voter Confidence Restoration Act repeals provisions of the law permitting same day registration; requires a legitimate excuse to vote absentee in limited cases like active duty military serving overseas . Anyone who meets the limited criteria for voting absentee will have to notarize their mail in ballot.
Voter are encouraged to vote in person on Election Day and present a valid government issued photo identification. The 45 day election season is completely eliminated. Absentee drop boxes are completely eliminated.
All ballots must be received by the general registrar by 7pm, the close of polls on election day in order to be counted.
This Act requires presentation of a form of identification containing a photograph in order to vote; repeals the provisions of law permitting a voter who does not have one of the required forms of identification to vote after signing a statement that he is the named registered voter he claims to be.
The Voter Confidence Restoration Act eliminates the use of electronic poll books and voting machines and requires that ballots be manually tabulated in order to determine the results of an election.
An amendment will be added to the bill to eliminate the use of Soros sponsored [Electronic Registration Information Center (ERIC)](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001lptxhaQT_idHd5p0T0Y5o6szSWfwSste8PeUNm7TOL34mW3J7QfYBt-sl-IUXIDPWfzKiKsDW-NRaHPrfXkE6xQVN9HR5lj4OVDo0yjte2gWNRx0i20yfj-HN3qLW_k64p5qof7rOHhekuSxZDoTww==&c=BzwnkIGP1A0lfVtTkUpq0RD7FR9wHvPtFc-mGevIpsh6MkFAxx7tlg==&ch=XWOAwuYUL2d1XEQTEqZJ1rwiabC5KeGWeX_g6DTs0Y3WmdAcyLY8Fw==)
"In 2020, many Virginians either went to the polls to cast a vote only to find out that for the first time since they registered to be a voter, that their sacred vote had already been cast. Election officials reported nefarious and questionable thumb drives that just happened to have enough votes to change the results of a Congressional race and the list goes on.
The bottom line is that beginning in 2020, many Virginians for the first time ever questioned the sanctity, processes and results of our elections and we've yet to restore that trust. After listening statewide to many Virginians, I'm doing something about it. Virginians don't trust voting computers and tabulators. They want hand counts and ballots that have a serial number for tracking purposes. They want a return to showing a government issued photo identification to vote and an end to mail-in ballots and drop boxes which do not protect the chain of custody of voter's sacred ballot. They also don't trust all the moving pieces involved with a 45 day voting season. It's time to return to a voting day with an actual deadline. Life is full of deadlines and Election Day shouldn't be the exception. " Senator Amanda Chase
THE VOTE SB 884 Elections; registration, absentee voting, and conduct of election.

| Chase Bill SJ224 Term Limits
---
| I've Introduced Term Limit Legislation Every Year for 8 Years and Every Year It's Killed by Politicians
---
| What legislature will vote for term limits? Apparently none.
Senator Chase introduced SJ224 a Constitutional amendment (first resolution); General Assembly; term limits. Limits members of the Senate to three full terms (12 years)and members of the House of Delegates to six full terms (12 years).
The limitations apply to service for both consecutive and non consecutive terms. Service for a partial term does not preclude serving the allowed number of full terms. In addition to any partial term, a person may serve 12 years in each house, or a total of 24 years in the General Assembly. The limits apply to terms of service beginning on and after the start of the 2024 Regular Session of the General Assembly.
About half of the Commonwealth and half of the country distrust our government. They would like to see more frequent turnover to prevent corruption and to increase accountability.
This bill allows for 12 years in each the Virginia House of Delegates and the Virginia Senate, which applies to consecutive or non consecutive terms, for a maximum total of 24 years in the General Assembly. While we want to ensure that our elected representatives run our legislature not the unelected bureaucracy that sees elected leaders come and go, career politicians should expire after 24 years.
We don't want too short of a term. This is what has happened in states like Florida where the term limit is too short. We do not want to empower or embolden the bureaucracy of each body and allow our legislature to be run by unelected officials, but we do want to ensure that legislators outstay their welcome.
We must ensure that legislators have reasonable term limits. Other states who have implemented term limits include Florida, Louisiana, Missouri, and 12 other states. Average term limit implemented elsewhere: 6 of the 15 states implement lifetime limits, ranging from 6 to 16 years; 9 of the 15 states implement consecutive limits ranging from 8-12 years Virginia has a tradition having a citizen legislature. We should be promoting the temporary nature of elected positions, rather than creating careers out of holding office.
THE VOTE SJ 224 Constitutional amendment; General Assembly, term limits (first reference).

SB791 The SAFE Act "Save Adolescents from Experimentation Act" Presented Tuesday by Senator Amanda Chase

Senator Amanda Chase presented SB791 the SAFE Act, otherwise known as the Save Adolescents from Experimentation Act, to prohibit permanent and irreversible experimental gender transition procedures on minors. Finally, the bill prohibits insurance companies from being forced to cover the tremendous cost of these experimental procedures and passing the cost along to the consumer. "Public school children, who still believe in Santa and the Easter Bunny, are being allowed to make permanent irreversible medical decisions that will affect them for the rest of their lives. This is outrageous given students can't even legally get tattoos or take aspirin on their own on school property. "

"It's time to put an end to "woke" doctors taking financial advantage of vulnerable parents and their children. "

| I do my best to represent the people in our district and across Virginia. You have nominated me to be your voice. Unfortunately, the chair of the committee was too hurried and more concerned with getting to a 7pm basketball practice that he was listening to this bill. I've included my arguments here that I was not able to present.
While it can be frustrating to combat opposing ideologies, you need me, that representative who will never give up no matter how difficult the challenge in the face of adversity that refuses to acknowledge the topics that are important to you.
Employers do not own you. They shouldn't be able to mandate experimental vaccines that are providing with every passing day to be injurious and deadly.
SB 792 was to ensure even private employers could not discriminate against anyone who chooses not to get the toxic jab. You should never have to chose between making a living and injecting something into your body that has no studies on long term effects and has caused so many deaths.
I was disappointed that Senator Peterson was more concerned about getting to his 7 o'clock basketball game than ensuring Virginians could get relief from overzealous employers. This is a life or death issue for many families.
Many Virginians Don't Want to Get the Covid Vaccine. Why are we forcing Virginians to get a vaccine against their will? Virginians are being forced to choose between getting a jab and keeping their job or losing everything. Here's what you need to know.
SB 792 COVID-19 immunization; prohibition on requirement, discrimination prohibited, civil penalty.

| I couldn't stay silent. SB 901 says it's a crime to leave a firearm in an unlocked, unattended vehicle. While I agree you should lock your car if you have to leave your gun behind, that doesn't mean it should be a crime should you forget. Why are we trying to legislate common sense? Maybe Democrats should stop passing laws that create gun free zones that force law abiding citizens to lock their guns in their car and instead allow them to carry on their person. The left wants you to disarm and be vulnerable. Why don't they instead eliminate gun free zones so we can keep our weapon on our person? It's better to keep your firearm on your person anyway.
Gun free zones are dangerous. They are respected by law abiding citizens only. Criminals are destined to attack. They create unsafe environments for those in attendance.

| Chase Bill SR87 The Life Begins at Conception Act
---
| Life Begins at Fertilization
---
|
SR87 Life Begins at Conception Act Presented this Friday by Senator Amanda Chase
This Act Recognizes that the Right to Life Begins at Conception
CHESTERFIELD, VA - Friday, February 3, Senator Amanda Chase will present SR87, otherwise known as the Life Begins at Conception Act, to recognize the science, that life begins at conception.
The American College of Pediatrics in March 2017 clearly defined the science; life begins at conception or fertilization:
"The predominance of human biological research confirms that human life begins at conception--fertilization.
At fertilization, the human being emerges as a whole, genetically distinct, individuated zygotic living human organism, a member of the species Homo sapiens, needing only the proper environment in order to grow and develop.
The difference between the individual in its adult stage and in its zygotic stage is one of form, not nature. This statement focuses on the scientific evidence of when an individual human life begins." "Regardless of an individual's political, ideological or religious belief, the science clearly states that life begins at conception or the moment of fertilization."
"While we may share passionate differences in opinion on whether a mother can abort innocent life and when, the fact still remains; life begins at fertilization."
"If we're going to advocate supporting the science, then we must recognize this simple fact before any other debate can begin." Senator Amanda Chase
VOTES
* 02/03/23 Senate: Passed by indefinitely in Rules by voice vote

| My desperate opponent Tina Ramirez falsely mentioned on The John Reid Show on WRVA that I was "kicked out of the Republican Senate Caucus" when in fact that is NOT true.
I WAS NOT KICKED OUT OF THE REPUBLICAN SENATE CAUCUS.
In fact, I left based on principle. I could not sit under a leader who introduces tax increases and gun control. Senator Tommy Norment consistently votes for gun control. The question is. Why is he still the Republican Minority Leader?
Sometimes leading means you have to take a bold strong stand and draw a line in the sand. It's important to me that we elect Republican leaders who follow the Republican Creed. Leaders should be the example to follow.
As a strong 2A suburban mom, I simply will not sit under leadership that introduces and votes for gun control. It's that important.
This decision was not made because of personalities or because I didn't like the leader personally, but based on principle. I could no longer in good conscience sit under a leader that I felt did not introduce bills consistent with the Republican Creed. So true to my word, I left the caucus.
I continue to interact and work with my colleagues, including the leader, to successfully represent my district even under a Democrat controlled Senate.
Just this past year, I worked with my colleagues to pass a bill that exempts veterans retirement benefits from state income tax up to $40,000. The leader and many other of my colleagues signed on as copatrons of the bill, which was signed into law by Governor Youngkin. I've also signed on as a copatron to Governor Youngkin's platform bills to support his agenda for Virginia.
It is difficult not just for me, but all of my Republican Senate colleagues with good bills our base would love to see pass, that don't make it out of committee. That's because the Virginia Senate is still controlled in all subcommittees, committees and the Senate floor by Democrats. If your bill makes it out of the Senate, it's a bill that Senate Democrats would have to like as well.
Think about it.
They do not share our values on election integrity, medical freedom, parental education choice and the list goes on. Finding common ground is difficult.
The Senate has been controlled by Senate Democrats 21 to 19 since 2020 and we just lost another seat in Virginia Beach after Jen Kiggans was elected to Congress. We're now 22 to 18.
Most good legislation is killed in subcommittee to include any good legislation that passes the House.
Most of my colleagues have the same issues I do with getting good bills out of Democrat Controlled Senate committees and subcommittees even with complete Republican support. We simply don't have the votes.
Tina Ramirez doesn't understand how the Senate committee structure works because she's never been in the Senate, nor held any elected office for that matter.
Not sitting on Democrat controlled committees has freed up my time and given me the flexibility to follow and speak on important bills that come up in any committee, not just the ones on which I served.
Because Democrats currently control all committees, Republicans have little input on the bills that pass out of committee. Democrats can pass any bill they want out of committee without the vote of a single Republican. And that's in every Senate committee.
I have more time to prepare for session and time to meet with constituents. Once re-elected and a new leader is chosen, I do plan on rejoining the Republican Senate Caucus.
You need experienced leadership who has successfully served and represented the citizens of Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia for almost 8 years.
You need a representative with the backbone to stand up to leadership when they go off the rails, not another status quo politician who does what they're told and doesn't stand up for our principles when they are being infringed upon.
Tell Tina to stop telling lies about Senator Amanda Chase. Tell her to stop running against Republicans and instead run against Democrats. If she were truly a conservative, she would support Amanda Chase.

clean_body: From the Desk of Senator Chase | | | | | | == | CUT TO THE CHASE WITH SENATOR CHASE Weekly Session Newsletter Week 4 of 7 | | | | | | WHAT HAPPENED THIS WEEK? | WEEK 4: GENERAL ASSEMBLY UPDATE | | | Senator Amanda Chase Senator Chase has represented Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia Counties in the Senate since first elected in 2015, almost 8 years. She has been rated as the most conservative Senator in Virginia by the American Conservative Union and has a lifetime score of 100% with the Family Foundation, VCDL and A with the NRA. She is also a champion in the General Assembly for pushing back against vaccine and mask mandates, calling for a full investigation into the 2020 Presidential Election, parental rights in education, medical freedom, protecting gun rights and help for our veterans. | 2023 SESSION WEEK 4 OVERVIEW "The Hunger Games" Dear Friends, It's been a tough week. I had to play catch up after being out sick with Covid for 3 days. But thanks to my hardworking team, we did it. I was back last Thursday presenting bills. Some days, we felt like we were living an episode out of The Hunger Games. Despite every obstacle that got thrown our way, with God's help, we prevailed. This week I presented all of my bills from last week plus the ones scheduled for this week. As of Friday, I will have presented all 19 bills just in time for crossover, which is next Wednesday, February 8. You can click a list of those bills. Fortunately, all of my bills were heard in the Senate. Unfortunately, they all died in Democrat controlled committees. In the Senate Privileges and Elections Committee, the Senate Health and Education Committee, the Judiciary Committee the committees are stacked against 9 Democrats to 6 Republicans or worse yet Senate Commerce and Labor with 12 Democrats to 3 Republicans. Clearly, Democrats do not support ending Covid mandates, school choice, protecting innocent life, protecting the 2nd Amendment or election integrity. In order to pass these bills, we're going to have to win back the Virginia Senate this year. The only clear solution is to elect more Republicans to the Virginia Senate this coming June 20 in the Republican Primary and on November 7 in the General Election. Currently, the Virginia Senate is 22 Democrats to 18 Republicans. We must regain control of the Senate if we're going to pass meaningful legislation that is important to you. All Senate bills must be heard before Tuesday at midnight, or they die. Beginning Wednesday, we will only vote on bills that passed out of the House. Beginning next Wednesday, my focus will be House bills. Unfortunately, all 19 of my bills died at the hands of Democrat controlled Senate Committees. This came as no surprise to us and was our expectation. While it's great to have your bills pass, it's more important in my opinion, to introduce bills that truly impact people's lives in a meaningful way. Otherwise, what's the point? Every time you present a bill, you are given an opportunity to educate and inform stakeholders, fellow legislators and move the needle closer to passing meaningful legislation once your party is back in control. Plus you are forcing votes on important issues, which is important right before election season. Even though I've been in the Senate for 8 years, I haven't forgotten who I represent. I represent you, not the special interest groups and lobbyists. All 19 of the bills that I introduced this year came from you. While we may have to wait next year to successfully advance these issues, we are primed and ready once we do take back the Senate. In this week's newsletter, I provide an overview of the fourth week of the 2023 General Assembly Session. I've presented well over 11 bills in the past 2 weeks. Included for you in this newsletter are overviews and even some videos of bills that I presented in committee along with comments I've made regarding certain bills on the Senate floor. There are links at the bottom of the newsletter that will help you follow various bills, legislators, committees and how to reach my office. If you'd like to stop by the General Assembly, I encourage you to reach out to my office below. We'd love to have you. If you call in advance we will do our best to find a time that I can personally meet with you. Also, if you'd like to help out, we're looking for people to collect signatures so that I can get my name on the ballot. I need 250 signatures. If you're interested, please reach out to Debbie at 804 393 2715. Please keep us in your prayers as we enter crossover next week. We hope to see you soon! | Chase Bills SB805 and SB1488 | Repealing the Unconstitutional Gun Grab of 2020 | | | | Senator Chase Presents SB 805 Reverse Gun Free Zones by Localities | | | Senator Chase Presents SB1488 Reverse Red Flag Laws | Senator Amanda Chase presented both SB1488 to Reverse Unconstitutional and Unamerican "Red Flag" Laws and SB805 to Reverse Locality Control of Gun Free Zones before the Senate Judiciary Committee Monday morning at 8am in the Senate Judiciary Committee. Both bills seek to reverse the Gun Grab that took place in 2020. Unfortunately, both bills died in the Senate Judiciary Committee with the help of Democrats and Republican Minority Leader Senator Tommy Norment who voted with Democrats in favor of gun control on both bills. SB1488 is a Chase sponsored bill to Reverse Unconstitutional "Red Flag" Laws which fail to recognize that the government should NOT seize weapons from people who haven't been convicted of a crime. "Individuals have a Constitutional right to protect the health and well being of both themselves and their family from violence that has overtaken many peaceful Virginia communities. Many suburban moms refuse to be victims in our crime ridden neighborhoods and choose to conceal carry to protect both themselves and their family. When seconds matter, we will do whatever it takes to protect our children from criminals. Once again, the Virginia Democrat Party violates the Constitution, restricts our gun rights and in 2020 passed unconstitutional laws. It's time to draw a line in the sand and take back what is ours." stated Senator Chase. Even the New York State Supreme Court agrees. Red Flag laws are unconstitutional. They allow the government to take away our guns without any due process for up to 14 days and potentially longer, placing the burden on the unsuspecting individual who has not committed a crime. This is unamerican. What happened to innocent until proven guilty? Red flag laws allow individuals who are not medically trained to deny an individual their basic fundamental right to protect themselves despite the fact that the person hasn't committed a crime," continued Senator Amanda Chase. The State Supreme Court judge in Rochester, New York ruled this past December that Red Flag laws are unconstitutional. "The Courts agreed with Colorado local district attorney Michael Allen who when the Democrats came after our gun rights in 2020 said, "Red flag laws are not only unconstitutional, they are a poor excuse to take people's guns and not designed in any way to address real mental health concerns.'" "It's time to honor the U.S. Constitution and strike these unconstitutional laws that are mischievous at best, allowing individuals to take away another individual's rights based on speculation and hearsay," stated Senator Chase. THE VOTE: SB 1488 Firearms; "Red Flag Laws" removal from persons posing substantial risk, penalties. 01/30/23 Senate: Passed by indefinitely (killed) in Judiciary (10-Y 5-N) (Y= yes to kill the bill) YEAS--Edwards, Deeds, Saslaw, Norment, Lucas, Petersen, Surovell, McClellan, Boysko, Morrissey--10. NAYS--Obenshain, McDougle, Stuart, Stanley, Peake--5. ABSTENTIONS--0. All Republicans voted to repeal "Red Flag Laws" except Democrats and Republican Minority Leader Senator Tommy Norment Senator Chase is also presented SB805 to reverse a Virginia law that was passed in 2020 by the Democrat controlled General Assembly which allows a locality to create gun free zones. Gun free zones disarm law abiding citizens who can no longer legally protect themselves. Democrat controlled cities across the state of Virginia have now passed a confusing and burdensome patchwork of regulations and ordinances which vary from city to city and county to county. This makes traveling difficult for even the most conscientious conceal carry holder to follow, increasing the risk that they could unintentionally and inadvertently violate the ordinance while traveling across the Commonwealth. We should not make it more difficult for law abiding citizens to follow the law. Gun free zones empower criminals and increase gun violence as criminals take advantage of disarmed law abiding citizens. Gun free zones increase the risk of guns being stolen from vehicles when conceal carry holders are forced to disarm and store their weapon in their vehicle to enter an event. Even guns locked in cases and stored in a vehicle can be stolen from an unattended vehicle. It is better for the conceal carry holder to be in possession of their weapon. "Gun free zones do nothing more than make law abiding citizens sitting ducks or fish in a barrel. Gun free zones make law abiding citizens more vulnerable and unable to protect themselves from criminals who have no regard for our laws. It doesn't matter how many gun control bills the legislature passes, they only embolden and empower criminals who don't follow restrictive gun laws, " stated Senator Amanda Chase. Senator Chase continued, "Over the past nearly two years, we have seen a troubling trend take place with Democrat controlled legislatures and localities across Virginia, who have mistreated and abused law enforcement while coddling violent murderers and criminals, while simultaneously misusing their authority to override both the U.S. Constitution and the Virginia Constitution to allow law abiding citizens to protect their family. Both state "'the people have the right of to keep and bear Arms, and that right shall not be infringed.'" Laws that infringe upon our federally protected Constitutional rights should be struck down. Virginia's very own Constitution protects the rights of Virginian's to protect themselves. The U.S. Constitution clearly states, "A well regulated Militia, being necessary to the security of a free State, the right of the people to keep and bear Arms, shall not be infringed." The Virginia Constitution in Article I of the Bill of Rights in Section 13 states, "That a well regulated militia, composed of the body of the people, trained to arms, is the proper, natural, and safe defense of a free state, therefore, the right of the people to keep and bear arms shall not be infringed; that standing armies, in time of peace, should be avoided as dangerous to liberty; and that in all cases the military should be under strict subordination to, and governed by, the civil power." "The Gun Grab of 2020 must be reversed. It violates our basic fundamental 2nd Amendment right to protect ourselves and I will work diligently until the Gun Grab of 2020 has been completely reversed," stated Senator Chase. THE VOTE SB 805 Firearms; control by localities of possession or carrying. 01/30/23 Senate: Passed by indefinitely (Killed) in Judiciary (10-Y 5-N) YEAS--Edwards, Deeds, Saslaw, Norment, Lucas, Petersen, Surovell, McClellan, Boysko, Morrissey--10. NAYS--Obenshain, McDougle, Stuart, Stanley, Peake--5. ABSTENTIONS--0. All Republicans voted to repeal "Red Flag Laws" except Democrats and Republican Senator and Minority Leader Tommy Norment | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | | Chase Bill SB849 Smart Meters | Making Sure Dominion Energy Asks You Before They Install a Smart Meter | Are Homeowners Being Fully Educated and Informed about the Potential Dangers of Smart Meters that Dominion Is Installing on Homes; Many Homeowners Are Saying Without Permission? SB849 Chase Bill Requires Dominion Energy to Receive Written Homeowner Consent Before Installing Smart Meters Senator Amanda Chase presented SB849 Relating to Public Utilities, Customer Consent to Install Wireless Smart Meters Monday Afternoon Before A Senate Commerce and Labor Committee. The Bill Died in Committee. Senator Amanda Chase presented == before the Senate Commerce and Labor Committee, Monday afternoon in an effort to ensure consumers reserve the right to decline without penalty wireless Smart Meters being installed on their home or business. The bill failed in committee 14 to 1. Note that every legislator on this committee receives thousand of dollars in donations from Dominion Energy. Senator Chase does not. Don't take my word for it. See for yourself using the links below. Legislators should not be accepting money from a utility monopoly whose SCC judges we appoint. SB849 Relating to Public Utilities, Customer Consent to Install Wireless Smart Meters recognizes that Dominion Energy or any other utility or 3rd party vendor should NOT install wireless Smart Meters without express written permission from the homeowner or business owner, who should be given full information about any potential dangers or risks associated with the meter and the right to decline without penalty if the customer decides against having the smart meter installed. SB849 Prohibits a public utility from installing a wireless smart meter, defined in the bill, on the premises of a customer without first obtaining such customer's consent to such installation. The bill requires a public utility, for any wireless smart meter installed by a public utility prior to July 1, 2023, to provide the customer an opportunity to consent to the previous installation. The bill requires a public utility to offer to remove a wireless smart meter without cost to the customer that was installed without obtaining the customer's consent. The bill prohibits a public utility from (i) discontinuing service to a customer who does not consent to the installation of a wireless smart meter; (ii) charging a fee, assessment, or higher rate to a customer who does not consent to the installation of a wireless smart meter; or (iii) providing or offering to provide discounted rates to a customer in exchange for obtaining the customer's consent to install a smart meter. "Customers have the right to make well informed and conscientious decisions that could potentially effect the health and well being of both themselves, their family, their employees and their customers from known and unknown risks associated with the use of wireless Smart Meters, without penalty. All we're asking for here is informed consent, full information and acceptable alternatives when the consumer declines. " Senator Amanda Chase THE VOTE SB 849 Public utilities; customer consent to install smart meter. 01/30/23 Senate: Passed by indefinitely (Killed) in Commerce and Labor (14-Y 1-N) YEAS--Saslaw, Norment, Newman, Obenshain, Lucas, Edwards, Deeds, Barker, Marsden, Ebbin, Lewis, Surovell, Mason, Bell--14. NAYS--Spruill--1. ABSTENTIONS--0. Thank Democrat Senator Lionell Spruill who was the only legislator to support this bill. Every legislator receives thousands of dollars in donations from Dominion Energy. Both Republicans and Democrats. | | | == | | | == | | | == | PRESS INTERVIEW 1140 WRVA THE JOHN REID MORNING SHOW The Voter Confidence Restoration Act and Reversing the Big Gun Grab of 2020 | | | == | | Women's Legislative Breakfast at the Governor's Mansion with The First Lady | Tuesday, Heather and I started our day off right by having breakfast with First Lady Suzanne Youngkin, who was kind enough to invite all women legislators to the Governor's Mansion for breakfast and fellowship. It was nice to have such a small group of ladies to include Suzanne's younger sister. We really were able to spend time catching up. Virginia is truly blessed to have a First Lady who loves the Lord and has such a heart for encouraging and inspiring women. | | Chase Bill SB884 The Voter Confidence Restoration Act | It's Time to Put the Guardrails Back on our Virginia Elections | Most Virginians Don't Trust That We Have Safe, Secure Elections SB884 Chase-sponsored Voter Confidence Restoration Act Attempts to Restore That Confidence Died on a Party Line Vote SB884 Chase Bill Restores Voter Confidence By Putting the Guardrails Back on Virginia's Elections to include Eliminating Voter Computers and Tabulators Senator Amanda Chase presented SB884 The Voter Confidence Restoration Act, This Tuesday Afternoon at the Capitol, Before a Senate Privileges & Elections Committee Virginia Senator Amanda Chase presented == An Election Integrity Omnibus bill called The Voter Confidence Restoration Act before the Senate Privileges and Elections Committee, Tuesday afternoon in an effort to restore Virginians' confidence in their elections. SB884 The Voter Confidence Restoration Act recognizes that most Virginians don't trust our election processes and therefore the results. This bill understands the importance of restoring that confidence by getting back to the basics of election integrity and ensuring safe, transparent elections that all voters can trust. SB884 addresses many of the concerns voters experienced during the 2020 Presidential Election and seeks to ensure these issues never happen again. The Voter Confidence Restoration Act repeals provisions of the law permitting same day registration; requires a legitimate excuse to vote absentee in limited cases like active duty military serving overseas . Anyone who meets the limited criteria for voting absentee will have to notarize their mail in ballot. Voter are encouraged to vote in person on Election Day and present a valid government issued photo identification. The 45 day election season is completely eliminated. Absentee drop boxes are completely eliminated. All ballots must be received by the general registrar by 7pm, the close of polls on election day in order to be counted. This Act requires presentation of a form of identification containing a photograph in order to vote; repeals the provisions of law permitting a voter who does not have one of the required forms of identification to vote after signing a statement that he is the named registered voter he claims to be. The Voter Confidence Restoration Act eliminates the use of electronic poll books and voting machines and requires that ballots be manually tabulated in order to determine the results of an election. An amendment will be added to the bill to eliminate the use of Soros sponsored == "In 2020, many Virginians either went to the polls to cast a vote only to find out that for the first time since they registered to be a voter, that their sacred vote had already been cast. Election officials reported nefarious and questionable thumb drives that just happened to have enough votes to change the results of a Congressional race and the list goes on. The bottom line is that beginning in 2020, many Virginians for the first time ever questioned the sanctity, processes and results of our elections and we've yet to restore that trust. After listening statewide to many Virginians, I'm doing something about it. Virginians don't trust voting computers and tabulators. They want hand counts and ballots that have a serial number for tracking purposes. They want a return to showing a government issued photo identification to vote and an end to mail-in ballots and drop boxes which do not protect the chain of custody of voter's sacred ballot. They also don't trust all the moving pieces involved with a 45 day voting season. It's time to return to a voting day with an actual deadline. Life is full of deadlines and Election Day shouldn't be the exception. " Senator Amanda Chase THE VOTE SB 884 Elections; registration, absentee voting, and conduct of election. 01/31/23 Senate: Passed by indefinitely (Died) in Privileges and Elections (9-Y 5-N) YEAS--Spruill, Howell, Deeds, Ebbin, Surovell, Mason, McClellan, Boysko, Bell--9. NAYS--Reeves, Ruff, Peake, McDougle, Hackworth--5. ABSTENTIONS--0. ABSENT- Vogel Died on a party line vote. | Chase Bill SJ224 Term Limits | I've Introduced Term Limit Legislation Every Year for 8 Years and Every Year It's Killed by Politicians | What legislature will vote for term limits? Apparently none. Senator Chase introduced SJ224 a Constitutional amendment (first resolution); General Assembly; term limits. Limits members of the Senate to three full terms (12 years)and members of the House of Delegates to six full terms (12 years). The limitations apply to service for both consecutive and non consecutive terms. Service for a partial term does not preclude serving the allowed number of full terms. In addition to any partial term, a person may serve 12 years in each house, or a total of 24 years in the General Assembly. The limits apply to terms of service beginning on and after the start of the 2024 Regular Session of the General Assembly. About half of the Commonwealth and half of the country distrust our government. They would like to see more frequent turnover to prevent corruption and to increase accountability. This bill allows for 12 years in each the Virginia House of Delegates and the Virginia Senate, which applies to consecutive or non consecutive terms, for a maximum total of 24 years in the General Assembly. While we want to ensure that our elected representatives run our legislature not the unelected bureaucracy that sees elected leaders come and go, career politicians should expire after 24 years. We don't want too short of a term. This is what has happened in states like Florida where the term limit is too short. We do not want to empower or embolden the bureaucracy of each body and allow our legislature to be run by unelected officials, but we do want to ensure that legislators outstay their welcome. We must ensure that legislators have reasonable term limits. Other states who have implemented term limits include Florida, Louisiana, Missouri, and 12 other states. Average term limit implemented elsewhere: 6 of the 15 states implement lifetime limits, ranging from 6 to 16 years; 9 of the 15 states implement consecutive limits ranging from 8-12 years Virginia has a tradition having a citizen legislature. We should be promoting the temporary nature of elected positions, rather than creating careers out of holding office. THE VOTE SJ 224 Constitutional amendment; General Assembly, term limits (first reference). 01/31/23 Senate: Passed by indefinitely (Killed) in Privileges and Elections (10-Y 0-N 3-A) YEAS--Spruill, Deeds, Reeves, Ebbin, McDougle, Surovell, Mason, McClellan, Boysko, Bell--10. NAYS--0. ABSTENTIONS--Ruff, Peake, Hackworth--3. | | | == | | | == | Chase Bill SB791 The SAFE Act | The Save Adolescents from Experimentation Act | SB791 The SAFE Act "Save Adolescents from Experimentation Act" Presented Tuesday by Senator Amanda Chase Prohibits permanent, irreversible and experimental radical gender transition medical procedures for minors by "Woke" Doctors Senator Amanda Chase presented SB791 the SAFE Act, otherwise known as the Save Adolescents from Experimentation Act, to prohibit permanent and irreversible experimental gender transition procedures on minors. Similar legislation sponsored by Arkansas Representative Lundstrum HB1570, which passed in 2021 and has been introduced in 21 other states. The bill died in a Senate Health Subcommittee and will be reported as such to the full Senate Health and Education Committee on Thursday. The bill will apply to minors only and prohibits experimental, permanent and irreversible sterilization and mutilation on minors in the name of "gender transition" procedures. It also prohibits the use of public funds for "transition procedures" for minors and establishes enforcement procedures for "woke" healthcare providers who chose financial gain over their Hippocratic oath to "do no harm." Finally, the bill prohibits insurance companies from being forced to cover the tremendous cost of these experimental procedures and passing the cost along to the consumer. "Public school children, who still believe in Santa and the Easter Bunny, are being allowed to make permanent irreversible medical decisions that will affect them for the rest of their lives. This is outrageous given students can't even legally get tattoos or take aspirin on their own on school property. " "Trusting parents are seeking help from trusted doctors only to find out these "woke" doctors are encouraging these experimental procedures and coercing parents into going along with expensive transition surgeries that they are falsely told will save their child's life. Ultimately many of these children detransition when they become adults and regret the decision." "It's time to put an end to "woke" doctors taking financial advantage of vulnerable parents and their children. " SB 791 Save Adolescents from Experimentation (SAFE) Act; established, health benefit plans, etc. 02/02/23 Senate: Passed by indefinitely (killed) in Education and Health (8-Y 5-N) Bill died on a "party line" vote All Republicans supported the bill YEAS--Lucas, Saslaw, Howell, Locke, Barker, Petersen, Hashmi, McPike--8. NAYS--Newman, Cosgrove, Suetterlein, Peake, Pillion--5. ABSTENTIONS--0. | | | == | | | == | Chase Bills SB792/SB833 No to Vaccine Mandates | It's Your Body; It Should Be Your Choice | == | I do my best to represent the people in our district and across Virginia. You have nominated me to be your voice. Unfortunately, the chair of the committee was too hurried and more concerned with getting to a 7pm basketball practice that he was listening to this bill. I've included my arguments here that I was not able to present. While it can be frustrating to combat opposing ideologies, you need me, that representative who will never give up no matter how difficult the challenge in the face of adversity that refuses to acknowledge the topics that are important to you. Employers do not own you. They shouldn't be able to mandate experimental vaccines that are providing with every passing day to be injurious and deadly. SB 792 was to ensure even private employers could not discriminate against anyone who chooses not to get the toxic jab. You should never have to chose between making a living and injecting something into your body that has no studies on long term effects and has caused so many deaths. I was disappointed that Senator Peterson was more concerned about getting to his 7 o'clock basketball game than ensuring Virginians could get relief from overzealous employers. This is a life or death issue for many families. Many Virginians Don't Want to Get the Covid Vaccine. Why are we forcing Virginians to get a vaccine against their will? Virginians are being forced to choose between getting a jab and keeping their job or losing everything. Here's what you need to know. SB 792 COVID-19 immunization; prohibition on requirement, discrimination prohibited, civil penalty. 02/02/23 Senate: Passed by indefinitely (killed) in Education and Health (8-Y 5-N) YEAS--Lucas, Saslaw, Howell, Locke, Barker, Petersen, Hashmi, McPike--8. NAYS--Newman, Cosgrove, Suetterlein, Peake, Pillion--5. ABSTENTIONS--0. All Republican Senators voted in favor. | Chase SB1315 No to Mask Mandates | Employers, Educators, Business Should Not Be Able To Mandate Masks | == | If you actually look at a box of paper covid-19 masks the manufacturer has printed on the mask DOES NOT PROTECT AGAINST VIRAL INFECTIONS Even the manufacturer has deemed these paper masks as ineffective against the spread of covid, and yet children across this country and across Virginia are being forced to wear these worthless cloth and paper masks. == commented on January 30 that he intends to May 11, 2023. Let's stop forcing people to wear masks. Studies show that these masks actually make you sick. The mask itself is a warm, moist dark environment which is not only conducive to growing bacteria, but the fibers in the mask itself have been found in the lungs and causing inflammation in the lungs. | SB 1315 Localities, public & private schools, higher educational institution, & employers; face coverings 02/02/23 Senate: Passed by indefinitely (killed) in Education and Health (15-Y 0-N) YEAS--Lucas, Saslaw, Howell, Newman, Locke, Barker, Petersen, Cosgrove, Dunnavant, Suetterlein, Peake, Edwards, Hashmi, Pillion, McPike--15. NAYS--0. ABSTENTIONS--0. | Pro-Life Day at the Capitol | | | Senator Chase speaks on the Senate Floor Against Gun Control | I couldn't stay silent. SB 901 says it's a crime to leave a firearm in an unlocked, unattended vehicle. While I agree you should lock your car if you have to leave your gun behind, that doesn't mean it should be a crime should you forget. Why are we trying to legislate common sense? Maybe Democrats should stop passing laws that create gun free zones that force law abiding citizens to lock their guns in their car and instead allow them to carry on their person. They created this problem with their ridiculous law that gives the localities the ability to create a patchwork of confusing regulations even the most conscientious gun owner struggle to follow. The left wants you to disarm and be vulnerable. Why don't they instead eliminate gun free zones so we can keep our weapon on our person? It's better to keep your firearm on your person anyway. Gun free zones are dangerous. They are respected by law abiding citizens only. Criminals are destined to attack. They create unsafe environments for those in attendance. | Chase Bill SR87 The Life Begins at Conception Act | Life Begins at Fertilization | SR87 Life Begins at Conception Act Presented this Friday by Senator Amanda Chase This Act Recognizes that the Right to Life Begins at Conception CHESTERFIELD, VA - Friday, February 3, Senator Amanda Chase will present SR87, otherwise known as the Life Begins at Conception Act, to recognize the science, that life begins at conception. The American College of Pediatrics in March 2017 clearly defined the science; life begins at conception or fertilization: "The predominance of human biological research confirms that human life begins at conception--fertilization. At fertilization, the human being emerges as a whole, genetically distinct, individuated zygotic living human organism, a member of the species Homo sapiens, needing only the proper environment in order to grow and develop. The difference between the individual in its adult stage and in its zygotic stage is one of form, not nature. This statement focuses on the scientific evidence of when an individual human life begins." "Regardless of an individual's political, ideological or religious belief, the science clearly states that life begins at conception or the moment of fertilization." "While we may share passionate differences in opinion on whether a mother can abort innocent life and when, the fact still remains; life begins at fertilization." "If we're going to advocate supporting the science, then we must recognize this simple fact before any other debate can begin." Senator Amanda Chase VOTES * 02/03/23 Senate: Passed by indefinitely in Rules by voice vote | | | == | | | == | JOIN US FEBRUARY 6 | | WITH YOUR GREAT HELP WE DID IT! And we did not solicit money from lobbyists or Political Action Committees. While I'm in session for the next 46 days I cannot raise money. Please save your donations until after we gavel out on Saturday, February 25. Meanwhile both of my opponents can continue to raise money while I'm busy working for you. Please hold your future donations until after adjournment on SATURDAY FEBRUARY 25 Please know that your personal handwritten note is coming, along with those who will receive a Chase Freedom hat. Know that I see your name and that I am so thankful for every donation you made and know that I could not do this without you. You will have to wait until Saturday, February 25 after we adjourn to donate again. I am in session from January 11 to February 25. Our next report will cover from January 1 to March 31, 2023. We will have from February 25 until March 31, 2023 or 35 days to raise money before our next report. | Please Sign My Petition to Get My Name on the Ballot | | In order to get my name on the ballot we must get 250 people to sign my petition. This is something I have to do every time I've run for re-election. If you'd like to sign my petition, please see me at church on Sunday in the foyer in between services or attend one of our upcoming events. Thanks to all who have already signed and asked to collect signatures for us. Please reach out to Jody at jody@chaseforva.com or call 804 912 6007. | SETTING THE RECORD STRAIGHT Desperate Tina Ramirez There she goes again... | My desperate opponent Tina Ramirez falsely mentioned on The John Reid Show on WRVA that I was "kicked out of the Republican Senate Caucus" when in fact that is NOT true. I WAS NOT KICKED OUT OF THE REPUBLICAN SENATE CAUCUS. In fact, I left based on principle. I could not sit under a leader who introduces tax increases and gun control. Senator Tommy Norment consistently votes for gun control. The question is. Why is he still the Republican Minority Leader? Sometimes leading means you have to take a bold strong stand and draw a line in the sand. It's important to me that we elect Republican leaders who follow the Republican Creed. Leaders should be the example to follow. As a strong 2A suburban mom, I simply will not sit under leadership that introduces and votes for gun control. It's that important. This decision was not made because of personalities or because I didn't like the leader personally, but based on principle. I could no longer in good conscience sit under a leader that I felt did not introduce bills consistent with the Republican Creed. So true to my word, I left the caucus. I continue to interact and work with my colleagues, including the leader, to successfully represent my district even under a Democrat controlled Senate. Just this past year, I worked with my colleagues to pass a bill that exempts veterans retirement benefits from state income tax up to $40,000. The leader and many other of my colleagues signed on as copatrons of the bill, which was signed into law by Governor Youngkin. I've also signed on as a copatron to Governor Youngkin's platform bills to support his agenda for Virginia. It is difficult not just for me, but all of my Republican Senate colleagues with good bills our base would love to see pass, that don't make it out of committee. That's because the Virginia Senate is still controlled in all subcommittees, committees and the Senate floor by Democrats. If your bill makes it out of the Senate, it's a bill that Senate Democrats would have to like as well. Think about it. They do not share our values on election integrity, medical freedom, parental education choice and the list goes on. Finding common ground is difficult. The Senate has been controlled by Senate Democrats 21 to 19 since 2020 and we just lost another seat in Virginia Beach after Jen Kiggans was elected to Congress. We're now 22 to 18. Most good legislation is killed in subcommittee to include any good legislation that passes the House. Most of my colleagues have the same issues I do with getting good bills out of Democrat Controlled Senate committees and subcommittees even with complete Republican support. We simply don't have the votes. Tina Ramirez doesn't understand how the Senate committee structure works because she's never been in the Senate, nor held any elected office for that matter. Not sitting on Democrat controlled committees has freed up my time and given me the flexibility to follow and speak on important bills that come up in any committee, not just the ones on which I served. Because Democrats currently control all committees, Republicans have little input on the bills that pass out of committee. Democrats can pass any bill they want out of committee without the vote of a single Republican. And that's in every Senate committee. I have more time to prepare for session and time to meet with constituents. Once re-elected and a new leader is chosen, I do plan on rejoining the Republican Senate Caucus. You need experienced leadership who has successfully served and represented the citizens of Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia for almost 8 years. You need a representative with the backbone to stand up to leadership when they go off the rails, not another status quo politician who does what they're told and doesn't stand up for our principles when they are being infringed upon. Tell Tina to stop telling lies about Senator Amanda Chase. Tell her to stop running against Republicans and instead run against Democrats. If she were truly a conservative, she would support Amanda Chase. | | | | | | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. subject: Have you seen what they’re doing to our kids?
body:
| |
---
| | [](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=f81fb6fe6f44457c8b5f7e218d96c9de&_e=xGqfiOfYz6mjMu7VQIUPvyz4l0FHSFiLYjIQndlhkZ33x7ovDdrDom8TsAypMXwT1oZ3sn2xXgu-ZhvKIjf-3t6JbAGV47VFTXKIYfLKlVpKibadh5aFj9HefqZD85GqztIUD9_qqx9ocCdEpCST3iI6T2XWEzeavdSXk97mF72cOLazPmZ3HXiMZbP1q_C1mNrZ5RQNhcP-oHfl51DKd57YTK4EUd2FI22O5rU9e2P6JH98Q1VLzcxucT1DuejB2OfX9razE9W7O2BduRMWHCDUX3qvaVfjjseqpcZywvY4n53qKgFVTQNBMEum0gNnqgrH7xmv2eber9cC56rEAbvP1UyzH4IJaYY1-W6p26Q483wsBeARW1evudEUvlSDYiF4hBS67pWyUTL2Kvgmcw%3D%3D)
---
| | Dear Fellow Conservative, My name is Harmeet Dhillon, the founder and Chief Executive Officer of the Center for American Liberty, and I’m asking for your immediate help to stop the radical Left’s medical mutilation of our children in the name of “gender dysphoria.”
---
| | [](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=f81fb6fe6f44457c8b5f7e218d96c9de&_e=xGqfiOfYz6mjMu7VQIUPvyz4l0FHSFiLYjIQndlhkZ33x7ovDdrDom8TsAypMXwT1oZ3sn2xXgu-ZhvKIjf-3t6JbAGV47VFTXKIYfLKlVpKibadh5aFj9HefqZD85GqztIUD9_qqx9ocCdEpCST3iI6T2XWEzeavdSXk97mF72cOLazPmZ3HXiMZbP1q_C1mNrZ5RQNhcP-oHfl51DKd57YTK4EUd2FI22O5rU9e2P6JH98Q1VLzcxucT1DuejBhuKsArpKj6fPTYejHDyMoo1CP2ldoBdc0oJ_Wllgjv6LO7Ct2sdWUhHbn8RsMugvnQrD6d-B1_NPcyRuvJ24eM4q6FD5TKNaugii3Z0Au7OvwNhUD7NPSr9X7j1Zxtq8mPSCBAAAJ8QGFFo8gFq4fg%3D%3D)
---
| We were recently on Tucker Carlson Tonight, announcing that we just launched a landmark case against the hospitals AND doctors who coerced Chloe Cole to transition to a “boy” at the young age of 13.
---
| | **In case you missed our announcement on Tucker’s show, let me quickly give you the details of our case:** As a young 13-year-old girl, doctors began transitioning Chloe to become a boy...
---
_This included putting her on experimental drugs like**Lupron** , a drug that has been used to chemically castrate violent sex offenders..._
---
_... Giving her constant testosterone injections..._ _... And removing both of her breasts in a double-mastectomy surgery at the age of 15._ **_How was this allowed to happen?_** It’s because the doctors and hospitals lied to the family –– including one powerful lie constantly used to scare parents into agreeing to this medical mutilation:
---
“Would you rather have a dead daughter or a live son?”
---
This lie is repeated to struggling families so doctors and hospitals can perform lucrative transgender medical experimentation on children... And I’m asking for your help today to put an end to it! [**Specifically, I’m asking you to make a one-time emergency donation of $25 today to hold the hospitals AND doctors responsible for their barbaric actions.**](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=f81fb6fe6f44457c8b5f7e218d96c9de&_e=xGqfiOfYz6mjMu7VQIUPvyz4l0FHSFiLYjIQndlhkZ33x7ovDdrDom8TsAypMXwT1oZ3sn2xXgu-ZhvKIjf-3t6JbAGV47VFTXKIYfLKlVpKibadh5aFj9HefqZD85GqztIUD9_qqx9ocCdEpCST3iI6T2XWEzeavdSXk97mF72cOLazPmZ3HXiMZbP1q_C1mNrZ5RQNhcP-oHfl51DKd57YTK4EUd2FI22O5rU9e2P6JH98Q1VLzcxucT1DuejBvelGWf2o_AXD_bCmgb22qNNImUuN2lwmvNjMETCkhp2TNsa5GslTxuW-U2IwgWP9_rErNYLBDyqoYWWT4Tq8QQmUCZ3-mzzGDozk1dnSJT8dYBJ0PW7DS8Sgc6TO_BTKCP1IptgWU1eyhRq0UUrbkA%3D%3D) Because until these predators are held responsible things will never change... And if we don’t act right away, more and more children will be put under the knife and experimented on. Medical facilities are actively promoting, encouraging, and advertising these barbaric treatments to minors. Chloe has suffered physically, socially, emotionally, and psychologically from their lies and experiments. Chloe also has no idea if she’ll ever experience the profound joy of motherhood... and if she is able to one day have kids, she knows she will not be able to breastfeed them. The medical experimentation performed on Chloe as a young child caused permanent and irreversible damage to her body and the predators that did this must be held accountable... ...and that’s where you come in. [**Please make an urgent donation of $25 today – but of course if you can give more, say $35, $50, $100, $250 or more I would be eternally grateful. **](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=f81fb6fe6f44457c8b5f7e218d96c9de&_e=xGqfiOfYz6mjMu7VQIUPvyz4l0FHSFiLYjIQndlhkZ33x7ovDdrDom8TsAypMXwT1oZ3sn2xXgu-ZhvKIjf-3t6JbAGV47VFTXKIYfLKlVpKibadh5aFj9HefqZD85GqztIUD9_qqx9ocCdEpCST3iI6T2XWEzeavdSXk97mF72cOLazPmZ3HXiMZbP1q_C1mNrZ5RQNhcP-oHfl51DKd57YTK4EUd2FI22O5rU9e2P6JH98Q1VLzcxucT1DuejBZQjMbyADBXqexB49NpeuHEuZY9Z1s0yYr2SPJMVRLprS4Tda5RnAv3cxMtRbQ-WjSOBHUFNuukHHzsNGyZ8fShTrWhwThYBReclN-dWXYUG6xNyGMR1ongtAFeozySOgHRWBvGkcc5Nc6PJMcogrvg%3D%3D) Whatever amount you’re able to give today will be used to hold these radical activists pushing this disgusting agenda on our children accountable. ** **
---
| | [](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=f81fb6fe6f44457c8b5f7e218d96c9de&_e=xGqfiOfYz6mjMu7VQIUPvyz4l0FHSFiLYjIQndlhkZ33x7ovDdrDom8TsAypMXwT1oZ3sn2xXgu-ZhvKIjf-3t6JbAGV47VFTXKIYfLKlVpKibadh5aFj9HefqZD85GqztIUD9_qqx9ocCdEpCST3iI6T2XWEzeavdSXk97mF72cOLazPmZ3HXiMZbP1q_C1mNrZ5RQNhcP-oHfl51DKd57YTK4EUd2FI22O5rU9e2P6JH98Q1VLzcxucT1DuejBvk_08lF1gQtA3ti8_S9131iZOMizHryswhp7p2ajyYySCt3c_5x20GxrJ7Wtr5a6ANpz0vplHt1HXoGyc6rUBSTh9kMCdaZ9byo-0sih4k5A00VTwR19h9zsQMsbsLaadLZ0IiqUswWaNXZuW5-_Iw%3D%3D)
---

clean_body: | | | | | | Dear Fellow Conservative, My name is Harmeet Dhillon, the founder and Chief Executive Officer of the Center for American Liberty, and I’m asking for your immediate help to stop the radical Left’s medical mutilation of our children in the name of “gender dysphoria.” | | | We were recently on Tucker Carlson Tonight, announcing that we just launched a landmark case against the hospitals AND doctors who coerced Chloe Cole to transition to a “boy” at the young age of 13. | | In case you missed our announcement on Tucker’s show, let me quickly give you the details of our case: As a young 13-year-old girl, doctors began transitioning Chloe to become a boy... This included putting her on experimental drugs likeLupron , a drug that has been used to chemically castrate violent sex offenders... ... Giving her constant testosterone injections... ... And removing both of her breasts in a double-mastectomy surgery at the age of 15. How was this allowed to happen? It’s because the doctors and hospitals lied to the family –– including one powerful lie constantly used to scare parents into agreeing to this medical mutilation: “Would you rather have a dead daughter or a live son?” This lie is repeated to struggling families so doctors and hospitals can perform lucrative transgender medical experimentation on children... And I’m asking for your help today to put an end to it! Because until these predators are held responsible things will never change... And if we don’t act right away, more and more children will be put under the knife and experimented on. Medical facilities are actively promoting, encouraging, and advertising these barbaric treatments to minors. Chloe has suffered physically, socially, emotionally, and psychologically from their lies and experiments. Chloe also has no idea if she’ll ever experience the profound joy of motherhood... and if she is able to one day have kids, she knows she will not be able to breastfeed them. The medical experimentation performed on Chloe as a young child caused permanent and irreversible damage to her body and the predators that did this must be held accountable... ...and that’s where you come in. Whatever amount you’re able to give today will be used to hold these radical activists pushing this disgusting agenda on our children accountable. | | | So please, do not delay. Chloe’s landmark case is the case that can put a stop to these “physicians” abusing and mutilating children... With your help we can save many more lives like Chloe's. | | Sincerely, | | | | | P.S. We must put an end to the medical mutilation happening to thousands of children across the country –– and winning Chloe Cole’s landmark case is how we stop them. Our case holds the hospitals AND doctors responsible for their experimentation and will ensure that these predators who abuse our children in the name of a radical Left-wing agenda think again. | | | | The Center for American Liberty is a 501(c)(3) charitable organization created to fight against growing anti-free speech and anti-civil liberties trends. © The Center for American Liberty | PO Box 2510, Leesburg, VA 20177 Sent to Reclaiming American Freedom subscribers. 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314

body:
| | | **BREAKING via _Wall Street Journal_ : "Affordable Care Act Enrollments Reach Record High"**
But even though MILLIONS of Americans are getting health insurance thanks to the Affordable Care Act, **_top Republicans promised to REPEAL the lifesaving law this year!_**
[](https://go.dscc.org/go/11810?ask=5&t=4&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20230204-BREAKING-ACARecord1QS-ETG-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20230204-BREAKING-ACARecord1QS-ETG-sl1&akid=9644%2E9716520%2EFB76EV)
**Tell us before midnight:
Do you approve of the Affordable Care Act?**
| | | [Yes](https://go.dscc.org/go/11810?ask=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20230204-BREAKING-ACARecord1QS-ETG-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20230204-BREAKING-ACARecord1QS-ETG-sl1&akid=9644%2E9716520%2EFB76EV)

Team -- Under President Biden, the Affordable Care Act just hit **RECORD-high enrollment** \-- _with more Americans getting health coverage through the law's insurance exchanges than EVER BEFORE!_

**But Republicans are _still_ doing everything they can to _attack_ the ACA and rip health care away from MILLIONS!**

We can’t let Republicans threaten our care, so we’ve launched an URGENT Live Poll to find out if Americans still support the Affordable Care Act -- **_but we’re 34 responses short in Pennsylvania! Please answer before MIDNIGHT:_**

[](https://go.dscc.org/go/11810?ask=5&t=7&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20230204-BREAKING-ACARecord1QS-ETG-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20230204-BREAKING-ACARecord1QS-ETG-sl1&akid=9644%2E9716520%2EFB76EV)
**Do you approve of the Affordable Care Act?**
| | [Yes](https://go.dscc.org/go/11810?ask=5&t=8&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20230204-BREAKING-ACARecord1QS-ETG-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20230204-BREAKING-ACARecord1QS-ETG-sl1&akid=9644%2E9716520%2EFB76EV)

| [No](https://go.dscc.org/go/11810?ask=5&t=9&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20230204-BREAKING-ACARecord1QS-ETG-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20230204-BREAKING-ACARecord1QS-ETG-sl1&akid=9644%2E9716520%2EFB76EV)
